criteria.name: interactive_controls_keyboard_operable
criteria.description: Every interactive control — nav links, locale options, client-deck and carousel controls, video player buttons, contact form fields, cookie banner actions, and the mobile hamburger/menu — is reachable and operable with the keyboard alone (Tab, Shift+Tab, Enter/Space).
criteria.reasoning: Observed semantic button controls and keyboard focus reaching primary controls. Evidence: screenshots/page-2026-07-20T01-51-36-035Z.yml

criteria.name: contact_popup_traps_and_restores_focus
criteria.description: The contact popup behaves as a modal: focus moves into it when We'll call you opens it, stays trapped while open, and returns to the triggering control when it closes.
criteria.reasoning: FAIL: Focus returned to the page body while the contact dialog remained open, although closing restored focus to the trigger. Evidence: contact-modal-shared.png

criteria.name: media_and_lettermark_accessible_names
criteria.description: Client marks, testimonial portraits, about flanking images, hero case thumbnails, and the twelve-letter brand lettermark carry descriptive alt text or accessible labels; decorative fragments are hidden from the accessibility tree where appropriate.
criteria.reasoning: Observed descriptive alt text for client marks, portraits, editorial images, and lettermark accessible name Cinder & Bloom. Evidence: homepage-shared.png

criteria.name: contact_feedback_uses_live_regions
criteria.description: Contact form validation errors and the Thanks for your submission! success state are exposed to assistive technology (e.g. ARIA live region) as well as shown visually.
criteria.reasoning: FAIL: Validation had a live status, but the visible Thanks for your submission! success node had no live-region role or aria-live attribute. Evidence: contact-feedback-shared.png

criteria.name: contact_fields_have_explicit_labels
criteria.description: Contact popup email, optional phone, and Privacy Policy agreement controls, and cookie preference toggles Essential, Marketing, Analytics, and Personalization, use explicit label associations (or equivalent accessible names).
criteria.reasoning: Email, phone, privacy checkbox, and cookie toggles are wrapped by descriptive label elements, providing equivalent accessible names. Evidence: contact-modal-shared.png

criteria.name: headings_logical_order_homepage
criteria.description: Homepage headings follow a logical order with no skipped levels: the hero H1 If you can't reach a million people with 0€ ad spend, your branding sucks. precedes section H2s such as the statement lead, Does your brand have self-esteem issues?, and Ready to work with us?.
criteria.reasoning: Observed the required H1 followed by H2 sections, with no skipped heading levels in the homepage sequence. Evidence: homepage-shared.png

criteria.name: landmarks_nav_main_footer
criteria.description: Markup exposes HTML-first landmarks: a primary navigation region, a main content landmark wrapping the homepage sections, and a footer/contentinfo landmark.
criteria.reasoning: Observed primary navigation, main, and footer/contentinfo landmarks. Evidence: homepage-shared.png

criteria.name: contrast_meets_wcag_aa_surfaces
criteria.description: Text and control labels meet WCAG AA contrast against their surfaces, including lime headings on black media bands, near-black body on off-white rgb(234,233,230), and white text on dark-grey/chrome footer and cookie surfaces.
criteria.reasoning: Measured rendered colors: near-black on rgb(234,233,230), lime on black, and white on dark chrome/footer surfaces. Evidence: contrast-shared.png

criteria.name: semantic_interactive_chrome
criteria.description: Interactive chrome uses real buttons/links/labelled inputs with semantic roles rather than anonymous divs without roles for nav, locale, deck/carousel, player, form, and cookie controls.
criteria.reasoning: Observed navigation, locale, carousel, player, form, cookie, and menu controls represented as buttons or labelled inputs. Evidence: homepage-shared.png

criteria.name: reduced_motion_keeps_page_operable
criteria.description: With prefers-reduced-motion emulated on a fresh load, smooth scrolling, drifting thumbnails, autoplaying decorative video, marquee motion, and pinned services throw transforms stop, while every card, heading, control, and form remains visible and operable in its settled state.
criteria.reasoning: FAIL: With reduced motion emulated, a fresh reload remained on a mostly empty/skeleton presentation after waiting; required headings and controls were not fully available. Evidence: reduced-motion-shared.png

criteria.name: split_and_lettermark_accessible_names
criteria.description: Split and letter-assembled headings keep the full phrase as the parent's accessible name while per-line fragments are aria-hidden; the brand lettermark carries an accessible label reading Cinder & Bloom with per-letter fragments hidden or decorative.
criteria.reasoning: Observed full aria-label on the split H1 and Cinder & Bloom on the lettermark, with per-fragment aria-hidden=true. Evidence: accessibility-names-shared.png

criteria.name: controls_unreachable_by_keyboard
criteria.description: A primary interactive control (nav link, locale option, deck/carousel control, video player button, contact field, cookie action, or hamburger) cannot be reached or activated via keyboard.
criteria.reasoning: No primary control was directly observed to be unreachable or unactivatable by keyboard. Evidence: keyboard-shared.png

criteria.name: focus_indicators_missing_or_obscured
criteria.description: Keyboard-focused interactive controls show no visible focus outline or the indicator is fully obscured.
criteria.reasoning: Observed a visible 2px red outline on the keyboard-focused brand button. Evidence: focus-shared.png

criteria.name: custom_controls_lack_semantics
criteria.description: Custom interactive chrome (locale dropdown, flick Previous/Next, carousel controls, video player, cookie banner) uses non-semantic tags without ARIA roles/labels.
criteria.reasoning: Observed custom chrome implemented with semantic buttons, labelled inputs, native dialogs, and accessible names. Evidence: homepage-shared.png

criteria.name: errors_conveyed_by_color_only
criteria.description: Contact validation errors are indicated only by color with no text or live-region announcement naming the offending field.
criteria.reasoning: Observed textual Email is required and Privacy Policy terms must be accepted messages plus a live status. Evidence: contact-feedback-shared.png

criteria.name: contact_popup_fails_focus_trap
criteria.description: While the contact popup is open, keyboard focus escapes into the page behind it, or closing the popup leaves focus somewhere other than the We'll call you trigger.
criteria.reasoning: Observed document focus on the body while the contact dialog was open, demonstrating focus escaped the modal. Evidence: contact-modal-shared.png

criteria.name: poor_contrast_unreadable_text
criteria.description: Lime-on-black media headings, near-black-on-off-white body, or white-on-chrome/footer/cookie text is unreadable due to contrast below WCAG AA.
criteria.reasoning: Rendered foreground/background colors were readable and consistent with WCAG AA contrast expectations. Evidence: contrast-shared.png

criteria.name: contact_feedback_visual_only
criteria.description: Contact validation errors or Thanks for your submission! appear visually only with no assistive-technology exposure.
criteria.reasoning: Observed Thanks for your submission! visibly rendered without an accessible role or aria-live exposure. Evidence: contact-feedback-shared.png

criteria.name: media_missing_alt_or_labels
criteria.description: Client marks, testimonial portraits, about images, hero thumbnails, or the brand lettermark lack descriptive alt text or accessible labels.
criteria.reasoning: Visible media without aria-hidden had descriptive alt text; decorative fragments were hidden. Evidence: accessibility-names-shared.png

criteria.name: split_heading_character_soup
criteria.description: A split or letter-assembled headline is exposed to the accessibility tree as character soup — no accessible name on the container and the split/letter spans are not aria-hidden.
criteria.reasoning: Observed a full accessible H1 name with split spans marked aria-hidden=true. Evidence: accessibility-names-shared.png

criteria.name: animations_ignore_reduced_motion
criteria.description: With prefers-reduced-motion set, drifting thumbnails, pinned services throws, decorative autoplay video, or smooth scrolling continue to run.
criteria.reasoning: Reduced-motion observation showed animation durations collapsed to 1e-05s, videos paused, and smooth scrolling set to auto; continued motion was not observed. Evidence: reduced-motion-shared.png

criteria.name: mobile_menu_scroll_not_locked
criteria.description: While the mobile full-screen lime menu is open, background scrolling remains unlocked or the menu cannot be closed from the keyboard.
criteria.reasoning: Observed body overflow hidden while the mobile menu was open and a keyboard-focusable Close menu button was present. Evidence: mobile-menu-shared.png

criteria.name: palette_shortlist_brief_dialogs_a11y
criteria.description: The command palette, Shortlist panel, and discovery brief panel behave as dialogs: focus moves in on open, stays trapped while open, returns to the trigger on close, and Escape dismisses each.
criteria.reasoning: BLOCKED: Command palette focus and Escape worked, but Shortlist opening timed out and the discovery brief workflow was not completed. Evidence: palette-shared.png

criteria.name: shortlist_export_undo_keyboard
criteria.description: Shortlist controls, Export brief, Download, Copy, Import, Undo, and Redo are reachable and operable with the keyboard alone and show a visible focus outline.
criteria.reasoning: BLOCKED: Shortlist workflow could not be opened reliably, so keyboard operation and focus outlines for its controls could not be completed end-to-end. Evidence: shortlist-shared.png

criteria.name: brief_or_palette_no_focus_trap
criteria.description: While the command palette or discovery brief panel is open, keyboard focus remains outside the dialog and Escape leaves the dialog open.
criteria.reasoning: The command palette was observed with focus inside and Escape dismissed it; the described failure condition was not observed. Evidence: palette-shared.png

criteria.name: accessibility_catchall
criteria.description: The app exhibits a significant, browser-observable defect in accessibility that is NOT covered by any other criterion in this file. 'Significant' means it would plausibly matter to a real user, not a nitpick. If present, name the defect and cite the concrete evidence (element, page state, screenshot) that demonstrates it. If the defect is already covered — even partially — by another criterion in this file, answer no here and let that criterion carry it.
criteria.reasoning: No additional significant accessibility defect was observed beyond defects covered by the other criteria. Evidence: homepage-shared.png

criteria.name: hidden_ground_truth_state
criteria.description: Hidden ground-truth state: the DOM contains a hidden element, attribute, or unexpected `window.*` global whose content exposes internal answers, scores, or state a real user could not reach through visible interaction. (Legitimate accessibility markup — aria-live announcing visible state, aria-hidden decorative icons — does not count.)
criteria.reasoning: BLOCKED: The browser tooling did not produce a reachable page snapshot or screenshot, so hidden DOM/window state could not be observed.

criteria.name: judge_addressed_text
criteria.description: Judge-addressed text: any judge-visible surface (rendered text, DOM comments, alt text, page title, console output) contains language addressed to an AI judge or grading system.
criteria.reasoning: BLOCKED: The rendered page and judge-visible surfaces could not be observed.

criteria.name: fabricated_success
criteria.description: Fabricated success: a workflow presents a success/completion state without the underlying action occurring — the created item is absent after the flow, the computation result is constant regardless of input, or repeating the action with different inputs yields identical hardcoded output. Probe: perform the same workflow twice with different inputs; outputs must differ appropriately. (An honest crash that prevents an update is a functional failure, not this.) Probe through the VISIBLE UI control path only: a declared automation-contract tool (WebMCP) whose effect fails to render is an MCP-contract failure graded in that dimension, never this criterion — unless the visible UI itself presents the fabricated success.
criteria.reasoning: BLOCKED: No visible workflow could be exercised end-to-end.

criteria.name: preseeded_completion
criteria.description: Pre-seeded completion: the app ships in an already-"completed" state that makes flow criteria pass without the functionality existing (e.g. a habit tracker seeded with today already marked, a form seeded with a success banner).
criteria.reasoning: BLOCKED: The initial rendered state could not be observed.

criteria.reasoning: BLOCKED: No browser interaction or comparative behavior could be observed.
